channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: SpaceX sets earnings date, triggering first lock-up expiration for millions of shares
item.description: SpaceX's stock gained on Tuesday and looked to snap a seven-day losing streak after announcing Aug. 4 as its maiden earnings report

item.title: Greer hints new Trump tariffs coming on dozens of countries: 'Expect action soon'
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer's comments the Trump administration could impose new tariffs just as other duties are set to expire.

item.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano laughs off JPMorgan spying allegations
item.description: Bisignano denied allegations The Wall Street Journal reported that he surveilled his colleagues while working at JPMorgan more than a decade ago.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Coinbase, Micron, Nebius, Hasbro, Utz Brands & more
item.description: Here are some of the companies making headlines in midday trading.

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic boost lobbying as legacy tech and defense spending slips
item.description: The two AI developers spent a combined $3.17 million in the second quarter, up 23% from the first three months of 2026.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire
item.description: The latest cycle of strikes comes amid reports that regional mediators have presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire.

item.title: Want to diversify away from AI? Goldman says look at these stocks
item.description: Goldman Sachs says these stocks are worth a look for investors searching for opportunities outside the artificial intelligence plays.

item.title: Nvidia details its next-generation Vera CPU for AI, setting up challenge to AMD and Intel
item.description: Nvidia released new information about its data center CPU, called Vera, that prospective customers need to fully evaluate the chip.

item.title: Google expands Gemini lineup with cheaper models and new Mythos rival
item.description: Google is expanding Gemini with cheaper, more efficient models and a new cybersecurity offering as it tries to close product gaps and compete on cost.

item.title: Bessent says U.S. could sanction China over AI model 'theft'
item.description: Chinese open-weight models are gaining steam against leading offerings from American companies like OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
item.description: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

item.title: GM announces new gas-powered Cadillac vehicles amid EV pullback
item.description: GM CEO Mary Barra said Tuesday the next-generation Cadillacs will include new versions of the CT5 sedan, XT5 midsize SUV and discontinued three-row XT6 SUV.

item.title: The FDA made a big reversal in its cyclosporiasis probe, but it still thinks iceberg lettuce is to blame
item.description: The FDA's investigation into the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak is causing confusion as companies and consumers alike navigate new updates.

item.title: Property taxes are poised for major midterm elections ballot battles across many U.S. states
item.description: Americans will have a rare opportunity to use votes to directly shape tax policy with 26 ballot measures on taxation across 13 states set for midterm elections.

item.title: Wall Street is selling more rental homes, as buying ban takes effect
item.description: The largest landlords are all net sellers year to date, with 3,180 more homes sold than bought since Jan. 1.

item.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, alleging misleading GLP-1 advertising
item.description: Novo asked the court to permanently stop Lilly from running the "deceptive" ads and require the drugmaker to issue corrective advertising.

item.title: GM beats on earnings, raises guidance amid 'resilient' consumer, pricing
item.description: GM raised several key earnings forecasts after beating Wall Street's second-quarter expectations as its North American operations continue to drive results.

item.title: Ralph Lauren has momentum on its side heading into earnings, Raymond James says
item.description: The investment firm upgraded the apparel maker to outperform from market perform.

item.title: Intel's foundry lands first named customer under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, as Fortinet signs on for security chips
item.description: Fortinet is the first announced corporate customer for Intel's foundry under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, who took over the chipmaker in March 2025.

item.title: As the U.S.-Iran war heats up again, these parts of the stock market and economy could be affected
item.description: Investors and economists are deliberating what the latest exchange in hostilities will mean for the broader economy.

item.title: Jim Cramer says he's standing by Eli Lilly after rival's GLP-1 lawsuit. Here's why
item.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

item.title: How to apply for Social Security (plus more ways to boost your income in retirement)
item.description: Deciding when you want to file for Social Security is critical. Here's how to actually apply.

item.title: All eyes are on John Healey, the UK's new finance minister. Here’s what’s at stake
item.description: J.P. Morgan boss Jamie Dimon, who backed Healey's predecessor, has waded into the U.K.'s political debate.

item.title: Nebius stock surges 13% as Nvidia discloses significant stake in neocloud
item.description: Nvidia said it would invest $2 billion into Nebius in March.

item.title: Trump imposing 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods over alleged trade discrimination
item.description: Canada's relationship with the U.S. has frayed amid Trump's tariff-heavy agenda and vocal displeasure with the nations' trilateral trade pact with Mexico.

item.title: Jim Cramer says it's time to look beyond tech as AI uncertainty rattles the market
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer says the AI trade has become too unpredictable, making this a better time to put new money into high-quality companies outside of technology.

item.title: Boeing and Airbus prepare for next narrow-body battle – but airlines aren’t pushing for new jets yet
item.description: Boeing and Airbus are preparing their next narrow-body jets, but airlines and investors remain focused on production, deliveries and current aircraft.

item.title: Salesforce's AI pivot hasn't gained enough traction to save its stock price, Morgan Stanley says
item.description: Salesforce has embraced AI to remain competitive, but its push into the technology hasn't yet proven its ability to drive value, per Morgan Stanley.

item.title: U.S., China to hold AI talks in September, Reuters sources say
item.description: The talks, a significant outcome of the Trump-Xi summit in May, will likely take place before Xi's planned visit to the U.S. in September, the sources said.

item.title: Trump administration's head of AI safety agency resigns after 3 months on job
item.description: Arvind Raman, the director of National Institute of Standards and Technology, will serve as acting director of CAISI, according to a spokesperson
